text: When setting project goals, it's important to follow the SMART criteria. SMART stands for Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. By defining clear deliverables, you establish what the project will produce. You should also break the work into milestones, which are key checkpoints to track progress. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with everyone involved. This helps prevent misunderstandings and keeps the project on track. With SMART goals, clear milestones, agreed deliverables, and strong stakeholder alignment, your project has a solid foundation for success.
